title: NDA Attempts checker : How to check how many attempts do you have left..
author: DefenceRoger Editorial
data: One of the most common questions asked by NDA aspirants is, 'How many NDA attempts do I have left?' Since the UPSC National Defence Academy (NDA) examination is conducted twice every year, many candidates get confused about whether they are eligible for the upcoming exam or how many opportunities they still have based on their age and educational qualification. To solve this problem, DefenceRoger has developed the NDA Attempt Calculator, a free online tool that instantly calculates your eligibility and remaining NDA attempts without requiring any manual calculations. The calculator is designed according to the latest UPSC NDA eligibility criteria and considers your Date of Birth, gender, educational qualification, and the official age limits announced in the latest NDA notification. Once you enter your details, the calculator automatically compares your age against the eligibility window for all upcoming NDA examinations and displays whether you are eligible, the NDA exams you can still apply for, your approximate remaining attempts before crossing the maximum age limit, and the reason if you are found ineligible. Unlike manually checking every UPSC notification, the calculator saves time and eliminates confusion by doing all the calculations instantly. If you want to determine your NDA eligibility manually, the process is straightforward but requires careful attention. First, check your Date of Birth against the age limits mentioned in the latest UPSC NDA notification. Then verify whether you have passed or are currently appearing in Class 12 from a recognized board. Candidates applying for the Army Wing must have passed or be appearing in Class 12, while candidates interested in the Air Force, Navy, or Naval Academy must have studied Physics and Mathematics in Class 12. You should also ensure that you satisfy the nationality, marital status, and physical standards prescribed by UPSC. Since the age criteria change with every notification, calculating eligibility manually for future NDA examinations can become confusing, which is why our calculator continuously checks your eligibility for upcoming sessions based on your Date of Birth. It is important to understand that there is no fixed limit on the number of NDA attempts imposed by UPSC. Instead, your total attempts depend entirely on your age eligibility. As long as you remain within the prescribed age limit and satisfy all other eligibility conditions, you may apply for every NDA examination conducted during that period. This means some candidates may get more opportunities than others depending on their Date of Birth. Our NDA Attempt Calculator automatically estimates your remaining opportunities so that you no longer have to guess whether you can apply again. If the calculator shows that you are eligible, you should begin preparing immediately by following the latest NDA syllabus, solving previous year question papers, practicing mock tests, and maintaining consistency in your studies. If you are not currently eligible, the calculator will clearly indicate the reason, helping you understand whether the limitation is due to age, educational qualification, or another eligibility requirement. DefenceRoger regularly updates the calculator whenever UPSC releases a new NDA notification, ensuring that every eligibility result is based on the latest official guidelines. Whether you are a first-time aspirant or someone planning future attempts, the NDA Attempt Calculator provides a quick, accurate, and reliable way to determine your eligibility and remaining opportunities within seconds.
